Welcome to Zewei’s Web
Hello! I am Zewei Kong, a Ph.D. Candidate at the University of Missouri. My work bridges the gap between rigorous statistical methodology and practical, data-driven application development.
My primary research interests lie in Bayesian statistics and data augmentation techniques. Currently, my research focuses on developing a new joint model designed to handle mixed-type data (Gaussian and binomial). The core motivation behind this work is to provide a unified, efficient, and simple framework where one currently does not exist.
Beyond theoretical research, I am highly passionate about building tools that make data actionable. I regularly use R and Python in VS Code to develop applications. Some of my recent software projects include TraffIQ and Melonu.
Whether it is discussing advanced Bayesian models, app development, or potential postdoctoral research opportunities, please feel free to contact me. I am always open to connecting with fellow researchers and developers.

View My CV
